title: Tissue fastening tool
abstract: A tissue fastening tool fastens a first biological tissue to a second biological tissue. The tissue fastening tool is made of two elastic wire rods, each shape set into a coil shape. Each of elastic wire rods are separately shape set, then ends of the rods are connected such that the second elastic wire rod coils back over the first elastic wire rod.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A tissue fastening tool which fastens a first biological tissue to a second biological tissue, comprising: a first elastic wire comprising: a first tissue fixing section is disposed at a first end of the first elastic wire and is configured to make contact with an interior wall of the first biological tissue; and a second tissue fixing section is disposed at a second end of the first wire and is configured to make contact with an interior wall of the second biological tissue; and a second elastic wire secured to one end of the first elastic wire at the first tissue fixing section, wherein the first and second elastic wires are configured to be in one of a coiled configuration and a non-coiled configuration, wherein when the second elastic wire forms a coiled shape in the coiled configuration, the second elastic wire has a central axis located within a diameter of the first elastic wire that is in a coiled shape in the coiled configuration. 2. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 1, wherein the coiled shape of the second elastic wire is wound in a longitudinal direction opposite than the coiled shape of the first elastic wire. 3. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 1 or claim 2, further comprising one or more welded joints that connect the first elastic wire to the second elastic wire. 4. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 3, wherein the one or more welded joints comprise a laser weld. 5. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 3, further comprising a sleeve configured to surround an end of the first elastic wire and an end of the second elastic wire. 6. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 5, wherein the sleeve comprises titanium. 7. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 5 or claim 6, wherein the sleeve is welded to the first and second elastic wires. 8. The tissue fastening tool according to any of Claims 5 through 7, wherein the sleeve comprises: a first diameter dimension that is greater than diameter dimensions of the first and second elastic wires when in a first temperature condition; and a second diameter dimension that is less than or equal to the diameter dimensions of the first and second elastic wires when in a second temperature condition, wherein the first temperature condition has a temperature value that is greater than a temperature value of the second temperature condition. 9. The tissue fastening tool according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first biological tissue comprises a wall of a duodenum and the second biological tissue comprises a wall of a common bile duct. 10. The tissue fastening tool according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the coiled shape of the second elastic wire is wound in a longitudinal direction opposite than the coiled shape of the first elastic wire, wherein the coil shape of the second elastic wire expands in diameter from the one end secured to the first elastic wire. 11. The tissue fastening tool according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising an atraumatic cap attached to one end of the first elastic wire. 12. The tissue fastening tool according to Claim 11, wherein the atraumatic cap is welded to the first elastic wire. 13. A method for making a tissue fastening tool which fastens a first biological tissue to an adjacent second biological tissue, the method comprising: shape setting a first elastic wire to form a first coiled shape having first and second ends; shape setting a second elastic wire to form a second coiled shape having first and second ends; and 

 attaching the second end of the first elastic wire to the first end of the second elastic wire, such that when the second elastic wire forms the second coiled shape, the second coiled shape has a central axis located within a diameter of the first coiled shape. 14. The method of Claim 13, wherein the second coiled shape is wound in a longitudinal direction opposite than the first coiled shape. 15. The method of Claim 13 or claim 14, wherein attaching comprises inserting at least a portion of the second end of the first elastic wire and at least a portion of the first end of the second elastic wire into a joining sleeve. 16. The method of Claim 15, wherein attaching comprises welding the first and second elastic wires to the joining sleeve. 17. The method of Claim 15 or claim 16, wherein inserting comprises: expanding a diameter dimension of the joining sleeve; inserting the second end of the first elastic wire and the first end of the second elastic wire into opposing ends of the expanded joining sleeve; and shrinking the diameter dimension of the joining sleeve. 18. The method of any of Claims 13 through 17, wherein the second coiled shape expands in diameter from the one end secured to the first elastic wire. 19. The method of any of Claims 13 through 18, further comprising attaching an atraumatic cap to one end of the first elastic wire. 20. The method of Claim 19, wherein attaching the atraumatic cap comprises welding the cap to the first elastic wire.
